question: Will the S&P 500 be above 6924.9999 on Apr 10, 2026 at 4pm EDT?
reasoning: The S&P 500 closed at 6,616.85 on April 7 and needs to reach 6,924.99 by April 10 - a gain of 4.7% in just 2-3 trading days. While today's ceasefire news is driving a 2.6% surge, the index would still need an additional 2% gain to hit the target. Given the significant geopolitical volatility and the short timeframe, a 35% probability seems reasonable despite today's rally.
key_factors: Current S&P 500 level at 6,616.85 requires 4.7% gain to reach target
U.S.-Iran ceasefire driving 2.6% surge today but still short of target
Only 2-3 trading days remaining until resolution
Significant geopolitical volatility and uncertainty despite ceasefire
risks: Ceasefire could break down leading to market selloff
Oil price volatility could continue affecting market sentiment
Technical resistance levels could prevent further gains
Short timeframe limits ability to reach target even with positive momentum
